factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Off (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: Trump announced framework agreement with Iran, canceling military strikes. Markets rallied hard Thursday; Nikkei +4%, South Korea Kospi +8%, gold +3%, oil fell to $85. Dow components (BA, CAT, transports) benefit from reduced supply-chain disruption risk.

factors.name: Hot US PPI Data
factors.summary: US Producer Price Index came in hotter than expected in May, per FXStreet and investinglive reports. This raises input-cost pressure for Dow industrials (CAT, HON, GE) and supports higher-for-longer rates narrative, capping multiple expansion.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY trades near 99.80, gaining ground on hot PPI and Middle East uncertainty. Strong USD headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (MSFT, GE, CAT, BA); multinational translation drag.

factors.name: Oil Price Stabilization
factors.summary: WTI steadies near $85 after Trump's Iran deal comments. Lower energy costs benefit transportation/logistics (UPS, FDX not in Dow but signals cost relief), but Chevron (CVX) in Dow sees margin compression; net modest tailwind given Dow's light energy weighting.

factors.name: Copper & Industrial Metals Rebound
factors.summary: Copper rallied from 3-week low after Trump signaled Iran deal. Signals improved demand outlook for CAT, CMI, industrial construction; easing recession fears among Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Rate Expectations / Fed Policy
factors.summary: No new FOMC data in window, but hot PPI raises probability of held rates longer. ECB hiked rates first time since 2023, supporting dollar. 10-year yield stable; no major shift to confirm dovish repricing yet.

factors.name: Deal Reality Check / Iran Skepticism
factors.summary: Multiple reports flag Iran Supreme Leader hasn't blessed deal, IRGC rhetoric remains combative, Hormuz tanker reportedly blocked. Trump's 39th 'deal' announcement history. De-escalation narrative faces credibility risk if deal falls apart over weekend.

factors.name: Corporate Profit Cycle & Earnings Resilience
factors.summary: Bloomberg report: corporate profits hit record highs vs. India economy; companies upbeat on demand and capex despite stock struggles. Dow's heavyweight earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, HD, V) historically stable; supports defensive holding.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO / Tech Spectacle Momentum
factors.summary: SpaceX $75B IPO (largest ever) and Nasdaq 100 additions (Rocket Lab, Astera, CoreWeave, AI names) generating retail/foreign investor flows. Positive sentiment spillover to tech-adjacent Dow names (MSFT, AAPL proximity), though Dow itself is industrials-heavy.

factors.name: Adobe CFO Exit / Execution Concerns
factors.summary: Adobe loses second top exec; stock at 7-year low. Signals management/strategy uncertainty in high-growth names; may spill into broader tech/software confidence, indirectly affecting MSFT sentiment.

screening.narrative: The Dow rallied +1.25% in 24h on Iran deal hopes and geopolitical de-escalation, with Nikkei and Kospi surging harder on reduced Middle East supply-chain risk. However, the rally is already ~90min into Phase 2 execution, and hot US PPI data is creating a countercurrent by reinforcing higher-for-longer rates and raising input-cost pressures on Dow industrials. Dollar strength (DXY 99.80) is a headwind for multinational revenue translation. The deal remains fragile—Iran's Supreme Leader has not officially blessed it, and IRGC rhetoric suggests leverage-building rather than surrender, raising overnight-to-weekend flip risk.
catalysts_ahead: Iran deal signing claim/confirmation (weekend, high flip risk)
PBOC monetary policy and yuan management (ongoing, China demand proxy)
RBA decision June 16 (holds expected; forward guidance on inflation/rates)
BOK rate decision next month (Shin hinting hikes; supports KRW, rate-sensitive Dow plays)
Dow component earnings: major banks (JPM, GS), healthcare (UNH), retail (HD) in coming weeks
US CPI/PCE inflation data (next releases post-PPI; rate-sensitive equities pivot point)
ISM Services PMI (mid-June; Dow cyclical confidence)
screening.risk_warning: Iran deal collapse or escalation (drone interception reported, Hormuz blockade threat) would reverse de-escalation rally and spike oil/VIX, hammering Dow's industrial leaders. Dollar unwinding on deal peace-and-calm could flip the headwind. Hot PPI repeat could force higher-rates repricing, capping multiples on dividend/value stocks (banks, utilities). Dow component earnings misses or guidance cuts would derail earnings resilience narrative.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in PHASE 2 (rally underway ~18h into catalyst); avoid chasing. Wait for pullback to 50,600–50,700 support or a close above 51,000 on Iran deal confirmation before adding. If deal falters overnight, 50,400–50,500 becomes tactical entry for reversal.
